description: verification-architecture.md §9 + §9b establish a reducer→shell command bus: feedReducer emits FeedCommand variants ('select-past-note', 'request-note-deletion', 'confirm-note-deletion', 'cancel-note-deletion', 'open-delete-modal', 'close-delete-modal') intended to be consumed by the impure shell. In the actual implementation the shell never reads reducer.commands for any of these variants — components call adapter.dispatchSelectPastNote / dispatchRequestNoteDeletion / dispatchConfirmNoteDeletion / dispatchCancelNoteDeletion directly inside their own click handlers (FeedRow.svelte:50-58, DeleteConfirmModal.svelte:27-35, DeletionFailureBanner.svelte:28-30). The reducer is exercised only for state mirroring on DomainSnapshotReceived (FeedList.svelte:43-47). Consequence: the FeedRowClicked / DeleteButtonClicked / DeleteConfirmed / DeleteCancelled / DeletionRetryClicked / DeletionBannerDismissed branches inside feedReducer.ts:50-97 are dead code at runtime, and PROP-FEED-005/006/035 'totality + biconditional' tests are proving correctness of unwired logic. The shell guard duplicates the reducer guard (isFeedRowClickBlocked) but since they are not reused via a single dispatch path, future drift between guard implementations is unprotected.
recommendation: Either (a) wire the components to dispatch through feedReducer (collect commands and translate to adapter calls), making the reducer the single source of truth for guards; or (b) remove the dead branches from feedReducer and from PROP-FEED-005/035 arbitraries to honestly reflect what the shell actually drives. Option (a) is preferable because it preserves the v-arch §9 contract.
